theory.intro: Large areas of land are measured in hectares (ha) and square kilometres (km$^2$) rather than square metres.
facts: $1\text{ ha} = 10\,000\text{ m}^2$ (a square $100$ m by $100$ m).
Use **ha** for paddocks, parks and sports grounds; use **km$^2$** for suburbs, forests and countries.
steps: Decide whether the area is land-sized (ha) or region-sized (km$^2$).
To convert m$^2$ to ha, divide by $10\,000$.
To convert ha to km$^2$, divide by $100$ (or m$^2$ to km$^2$ divide by $1\,000\,000$).
